concerns.text: FR-005 mandates a 'Wilcoxon signed-rank test' on accuracy distributions. The tasks (T027) and plan explicitly implement 'McNemar's test' (for categorical data). No task exists to implement the required Wilcoxon test, leaving a critical functional requirement unaddressed.

FR-008 requires a sensitivity analysis across 'model sizes'. T014 implements sensitivity analysis but the plan constraints and task description fix the model to 'distilbert-base-uncased' only. No task exists to implement model swapping or analysis across different model sizes, making FR-008 unimplementable as written.

SC-002 defines hallucination as 'incorrect terminal command execution OR state misinterpretation (<90% string similarity)'. T026 only describes calculating hallucination rates via 'string similarity < 90%'. No task exists to detect or log 'incorrect terminal command execution', leaving half of the success criterion unmeasured.

T006 references generating 'Terminal-Bench-Evo' dataset. The spec and plan clarify this dataset is synthetic and generated by code, not a pre-existing external download. While the task description is technically correct (generating it), the name 'Terminal-Bench-Evo' might imply an external fetch. Consider clarifying in the task description that this is a synthetic generator to avoid confusion with external datasets.
